summary: The normal GFR is approximately 125 mL/min (180 litres/day) and is estimated clinically by creatinine clearance.
The normal GFR is approximately 60 mL/min (90 litres/day).
GFR is best estimated by urea clearance rather than creatinine clearance.
The proximal convoluted tubule reabsorbs approximately 65-70% of filtered sodium, water, and all filtered glucose and amino acids.
The distal convoluted tubule reabsorbs 65-70% of filtered sodium and water.
The loop of Henle reabsorbs all filtered glucose and amino acids.
ADH acts on the collecting duct to increase water reabsorption by inserting aquaporin-2 channels.
ADH acts on the proximal tubule to increase sodium reabsorption.
ADH increases water reabsorption by inhibiting aquaporin channels.
Aldosterone acts on the distal tubule and collecting duct to increase sodium reabsorption and potassium secretion.
Aldosterone acts on the proximal tubule to increase glucose reabsorption.
Aldosterone decreases sodium reabsorption and increases potassium reabsorption.
The juxtaglomerular apparatus regulates renin secretion in response to decreased renal perfusion pressure and decreased sodium delivery.
The juxtaglomerular apparatus regulates ADH secretion in the posterior pituitary.
The juxtaglomerular apparatus is located in the proximal convoluted tubule.
The countercurrent multiplier in the loop of Henle creates a medullary osmotic gradient essential for urine concentration.
The countercurrent multiplier operates in the proximal tubule.
The countercurrent mechanism dilutes the medullary interstitium.
Afferent arteriolar constriction decreases GFR, while efferent arteriolar constriction increases GFR.
Afferent arteriolar constriction increases GFR.
Both afferent and efferent arteriolar constriction decrease GFR equally.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Revise tubular function and hormonal regulation.
